  "description": "Imperial Gate School is a top-notch private Nursery and Primary School located in a serene environment in Lekki Phase One.We value the growth of every child, hence, our commitment to partner with the home in ensuring a wholesome learning experience for all enrolled children.

Our curriculum is a blend of the English National Curriculum and the Nigerian curriculum with strong emphasis on morals and instilling a sense of responsibility in each child.&nbsp;

We are&nbsp;&nbsp;recruiting to fill the position below:

&nbsp;Job Position: Human Resource and Admin Manager

Job Location: Lekki, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities


	Acting as a strategic partner in ensuring the right talents are available and are aligned with the school&#39;s goals
	Spearheading staff recruitment, selection, and onboarding orientation
	Managing the school&#39;s performance management system, monitoring KPIs attainment
	Benefits administration and management; pensions, HMO, and Allowances
	Responding to daily HR inquiries and providing required support
	Organizing staff social events
	Liaising with vendors, contractors and artisans; negotiating the best rates for school purchases
	General oversight for the Admin and Support teams for optimum performance
	Providing all-round administrative support to the academic team in the delivery of teaching and learning


Requirements


	A Bachelor&#39;s Degree (minimum)
	Previous experience as School HR
	HR Certification
	Creativity
	Strong communication skills (oral and written).


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their applications including CV and cover letter to: hr@imperialgateschool.com using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

Human Resource and Admin Manager Job at Imperial Gate School




        
    

        
        Imperial Gate School is a top-notch private Nursery and Primary School located in a serene environment in Lekki Phase One.We value the growth of every child, hence, our commitment to partner with the home in ensuring a wholesome learning experience for all enrolled children.

Our curriculum is a blend of the English National Curriculum and the Nigerian curriculum with strong emphasis on morals and instilling a sense of responsibility in each child. 

We are  recruiting to fill the position below:

 Job Position: Human Resource and Admin Manager

Job Location: Lekki, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities
     

            
    




	Acting as a strategic partner in ensuring the right talents are available and are aligned with the school's goals
	Spearheading staff recruitment, selection, and onboarding orientation
	Managing the school's performance management system, monitoring KPIs attainment
	Benefits administration and management; pensions, HMO, and Allowances
	Responding to daily HR inquiries and providing required support
	Organizing staff social events
	Liaising with vendors, contractors and artisans; negotiating the best rates for school purchases
	General oversight for the Admin and Support teams for optimum performance
	Providing all-round administrative support to the academic team in the delivery of teaching and learning


Requirements


	A Bachelor's Degree (minimum)
	Previous experience as School HR
	HR Certification
	Creativity
	Strong communication skills (oral and written).


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their applications including CV and cover letter to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Finance Manager",
  "description": "Phillips Outsourcing Limited has been in existence for over 16 years. We have a vast experience in the outsourcing business as we currently manage resources for major clients in all sectors of the Nigerian economy. In addition, our prolific manpower cuts across the 36 states in Nigeria. After successfully gaining ground in all the 36 states of the federation, we are gradually spreading our tentacles to other African Nations. We are currently registered to also do business in Ghana. Our framework is developed to fully support the businesses of our clients, ensuring continuous improvement of the outsourced services. This ultimately thrusts our clients to achieve their business objectives.

We are&nbsp;&nbsp;recruiting to fill the position below:

&nbsp;Job Position: Finance Manager

Job Location: Yaba, Lagos
Employment Type: Full Time
Industry: Manufacturing

Responsibilities


	Maintain accurate, up-to-date accounting records at all times
	Ensure all transactions are correctly recorded, coded, and reconciled
	Prepare monthly management accounts: P&amp;L, Balance Sheet, and Cash Flow statements
	Close monthly accounts within agreed timelines, consistently and without follow-up
	Prepare weekly and monthly finance reports for the Managing Director
	Conduct Budget vs Actual analysis and clearly explain key variances
	Identify and communicate financial risks, cost trends, and revenue opportunities
	Provide actionable recommendations that directly support business decisions
	Present financial performance in a clear, structured format accessible to non-finance stakeholders
	Build and maintain accurate product and service costing models
	Conduct product-level and business-unit profitability analysis
	Identify activities, products, or SKUs operating below target margins
	Recommend and track actions to improve margins and overall financial performance
	Support all tax filings and remittances, including CIT, VAT, WHT, and PAYE with external auditor.
	Support timely submission of all returns to FIRS and relevant state tax authorities
	Manage pension remittances in line with PENCOM regulations
	Liaise with external tax advisors and regulatory bodies as required
	Keep management informed of tax obligations, deadlines, and any changes in tax legislation that affect the business
	Maintain the company&rsquo;s books in a state of audit readiness at all times
	Coordinate and manage the annual external audit process end-to-end.


Requirements


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Accounting, Finance, Economics, or a related field
	Professional qualification - CA, ACCA, or ICAN is required or near completion
	4-5 years of experience in Finance, Accounting, or Financial Management
	Demonstrable experience managing tax compliance (CIT, VAT, WHT, PAYE) in Nigeria
	Experience coordinating external audits
	Strong understanding of Nigerian tax law and statutory reporting requirements
	Advanced Microsoft Excel skills; experience with accounting software (e.g QuickBooks, or equivalent)
	Proven ability to operate independently and manage a full finance function without a team
	High attention to detail, strong ownership mindset, and consistent delivery without supervision
	Strong communication skills &mdash; able to translate financial data into clear management insights
	Experience in retail, FMCG, manufacturing, e-commerce, or inventory-based businesses is a strong advantage.


Salary
N550,000 gross per month.",

Finance Manager Job at Phillips Outsourcing Limited




        
    

        
        Phillips Outsourcing Limited has been in existence for over 16 years. We have a vast experience in the outsourcing business as we currently manage resources for major clients in all sectors of the Nigerian economy. In addition, our prolific manpower cuts across the 36 states in Nigeria. After successfully gaining ground in all the 36 states of the federation, we are gradually spreading our tentacles to other African Nations. We are currently registered to also do business in Ghana. Our framework is developed to fully support the businesses of our clients, ensuring continuous improvement of the outsourced services. This ultimately thrusts our clients to achieve their business objectives.
     

            
    



We are  recruiting to fill the position below:

 Job Position: Finance Manager

Job Location: Yaba, Lagos
Employment Type: Full Time
Industry: Manufacturing

Responsibilities


	Maintain accurate, up-to-date accounting records at all times
	Ensure all transactions are correctly recorded, coded, and reconciled
	Prepare monthly management accounts: P&L, Balance Sheet, and Cash Flow statements
	Close monthly accounts within agreed timelines, consistently and without follow-up
	Prepare weekly and monthly finance reports for the Managing Director
	Conduct Budget vs Actual analysis and clearly explain key variances
	Identify and communicate financial risks, cost trends, and revenue opportunities
	Provide actionable recommendations that directly support business decisions
	Present financial performance in a clear, structured format accessible to non-finance stakeholders
	Build and maintain accurate product and service costing models
	Conduct product-level and business-unit profitability analysis
	Identify activities, products, or SKUs operating below target margins
	Recommend and track actions to improve margins and overall financial performance
	Support all tax filings and remittances, including CIT, VAT, WHT, and PAYE with external auditor.
	Support timely submission of all returns to FIRS and relevant state tax authorities
	Manage pension remittances in line with PENCOM regulations
	Liaise with external tax advisors and regulatory bodies as required
	Keep management informed of tax obligations, deadlines, and any changes in tax legislation that affect the business
	Maintain the company’s books in a state of audit readiness at all times
	Coordinate and manage the annual external audit process end-to-end.


Requirements


	Bachelor’s Degree in Accounting, Finance, Economics, or a related field
	Professional qualification - CA, ACCA, or ICAN is required or near completion
	4-5 years of experience in Finance, Accounting, or Financial Management
	Demonstrable experience managing tax compliance (CIT, VAT, WHT, PAYE) in Nigeria
	Experience coordinating external audits
	Strong understanding of Nigerian tax law and statutory reporting requirements
	Advanced Microsoft Excel skills; experience with accounting software (e.g QuickBooks, or equivalent)
	Proven ability to operate independently and manage a full finance function without a team
	High attention to detail, strong ownership mindset, and consistent delivery without supervision
	Strong communication skills — able to translate financial data into clear management insights
	Experience in retail, FMCG, manufacturing, e-commerce, or inventory-based businesses is a strong advantage.


Salary
N550,000 gross per month.    

    

        Click here to Apply

  "@context": "https://schema.org",

  "title": "Procurement Executive",
  "description": "Fort Hermon Limited, We are a dynamic engineering and construction company specializing in delivering high-quality infrastructure, building, and industrial projects. With a focus on innovation, safety, and technical excellence, we provide end-to-end solutions that meet global standards and exceed client expectations.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Procurement Executive

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description


	We are seeking a highly analytical and experienced Procurement Specialist to join our civil construction team. In this role, you will lead the sourcing, negotiation, and acquisition of construction materials, equipment, and subcontractor services.
	You will ensure all project procurement is cost-effective, high-quality, and delivered on schedule to keep our civil engineering projects running smoothly.


Key Responsibilities


	Sourcing &amp; Vendor Management: Identify, evaluate, and onboard reliable suppliers and subcontractors for civil construction materials (e.g., cement, steel, aggregates, asphalt, piping) and heavy machinery/plant hire.
	Cost Estimation &amp; BOQ Review: Analyze Bills of Quantities (BOQs) and technical drawings to estimate material quantities, prepare cost comparisons, and ensure alignment with project budgets.
	Negotiation &amp; Contract Management: Negotiate pricing, payment terms, and delivery schedules with suppliers and subcontractors to secure the best commercial terms without compromising quality or safety.
	Purchase Order (PO) Processing: Prepare, issue, and track purchase orders, ensuring all documentation is accurate and compliant with company policies.
	Logistics &amp; Delivery Coordination: Coordinate with site managers, logistics teams, and suppliers to schedule timely deliveries to various construction sites, minimizing project downtime.
	Market Analysis: Monitor market trends, price fluctuations of construction materials, and supply chain risks to proactively develop cost-saving sourcing strategies.
	Quality Assurance: Collaborate with project engineers to ensure all procured materials meet required technical specifications, industry standards, and regulatory compliance.


Requirements and Qualifications


	Education: Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree or HND in Purchasing and Supply Chain Management, Civil Engineering, Quantity Surveying, Building Technology, or a related field.
	Experience: Minimum of 5 - 10 years of proven experience as a Procurement Specialist or Buyer specifically within the civil construction or civil engineering industry.
	Added Advantage: A background or formal training in Quantity Surveying (QS) is highly desirable and will be given strong preference.
	Technical Skills: Strong ability to read construction drawings, interpret Bills of Quantities (BOQ), and use procurement or ERP software.
	Key Competencies: Exceptional negotiation, contract management, analytical, and communication skills.
	Attributes: Highly organized, detail-oriented, and capable of working under pressure to meet tight project deadlines across multiple construction sites.


Salary
N350,000 - N500,000 per month.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Inetersted and qualified candidates should send their CV and Cover letter to: vineonyido@gmail.com using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Procurement Executive Job at Fort Hermon Limited




        
    

        
        Fort Hermon Limited, We are a dynamic engineering and construction company specializing in delivering high-quality infrastructure, building, and industrial projects. With a focus on innovation, safety, and technical excellence, we provide end-to-end solutions that meet global standards and exceed client expectations.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Procurement Executive

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description
     

            
    




	We are seeking a highly analytical and experienced Procurement Specialist to join our civil construction team. In this role, you will lead the sourcing, negotiation, and acquisition of construction materials, equipment, and subcontractor services.
	You will ensure all project procurement is cost-effective, high-quality, and delivered on schedule to keep our civil engineering projects running smoothly.


Key Responsibilities


	Sourcing & Vendor Management: Identify, evaluate, and onboard reliable suppliers and subcontractors for civil construction materials (e.g., cement, steel, aggregates, asphalt, piping) and heavy machinery/plant hire.
	Cost Estimation & BOQ Review: Analyze Bills of Quantities (BOQs) and technical drawings to estimate material quantities, prepare cost comparisons, and ensure alignment with project budgets.
	Negotiation & Contract Management: Negotiate pricing, payment terms, and delivery schedules with suppliers and subcontractors to secure the best commercial terms without compromising quality or safety.
	Purchase Order (PO) Processing: Prepare, issue, and track purchase orders, ensuring all documentation is accurate and compliant with company policies.
	Logistics & Delivery Coordination: Coordinate with site managers, logistics teams, and suppliers to schedule timely deliveries to various construction sites, minimizing project downtime.
	Market Analysis: Monitor market trends, price fluctuations of construction materials, and supply chain risks to proactively develop cost-saving sourcing strategies.
	Quality Assurance: Collaborate with project engineers to ensure all procured materials meet required technical specifications, industry standards, and regulatory compliance.


Requirements and Qualifications


	Education: Bachelor’s Degree or HND in Purchasing and Supply Chain Management, Civil Engineering, Quantity Surveying, Building Technology, or a related field.
	Experience: Minimum of 5 - 10 years of proven experience as a Procurement Specialist or Buyer specifically within the civil construction or civil engineering industry.
	Added Advantage: A background or formal training in Quantity Surveying (QS) is highly desirable and will be given strong preference.
	Technical Skills: Strong ability to read construction drawings, interpret Bills of Quantities (BOQ), and use procurement or ERP software.
	Key Competencies: Exceptional negotiation, contract management, analytical, and communication skills.
	Attributes: Highly organized, detail-oriented, and capable of working under pressure to meet tight project deadlines across multiple construction sites.


Salary
N350,000 - N500,000 per month.

Method of Application 
Inetersted and qualified candidates should send their CV and Cover letter to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Procurement Officer",
  "description": "Odixcity Consulting is an International Hr consulting and procurement firm. With expertise in Recruitment &amp; Selection, Performance management, Training &amp; Development, Compensation and benefits as well as Outsourcing of goods and services for organizations.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Procurement Officer

Job Location: Port Harcourt, Rivers
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are currently seeking a proactive Procurement Officer to manage end-to-end purchasing activities in our organization. The ideal candidate must have hands-on, proven experience sourcing materials and negotiating vendor contracts within the oil and gas servicing, manufacturing, or marine industries.
	As a Procurement Officer, you will oversee a vital part of our supply chain by evaluating suppliers, negotiating terms, and ensuring the cost-effective procurement of technical equipment, materials, and services. You will play a key role in avoiding operational delays by ensuring that all deliveries meet original specifications and strict industry quality standards.


Job Responsibilities


	Sourcing &amp; Tendering: Prepare proposals, request RFQs (Request for Quotation), and source reliable domestic and foreign vendors.
	Negotiation &amp; Contracting: Negotiate purchase terms, pricing, and delivery schedules to optimize company costs.
	Purchase Orders (POs): Prepare and process POs accurately in accordance with company policies and relevant procurement software.
	Logistics &amp; Expediting: Track placed orders from initial purchase to final delivery in Port Harcourt, ensuring timely arrivals without stockouts.
	Quality Control: Inspect and evaluate the quality of purchased items, resolving any discrepancies or shortcomings with suppliers.
	Record Keeping: Maintain meticulous records of purchasing activities, pricing data, supplier databases, and vendor performance.


Requirements &amp; Skills


	Education: B.Sc / HND in Supply Chain Management, Business Administration, Engineering, or a related discipline.
	Experience: 2&ndash;3 years of proven experience in procurement/supply chain within the oil and gas servicing, manufacturing, or marine industry.
	Technical Knowledge: Familiarity with oilfield/marine equipment, materials, and service contracts.
	Regulatory Familiarity: Good understanding of local industry content requirements (e.g., Nigerian Content policies) is a distinct advantage.
	Skills: Exceptional negotiation, analytical, and communication capabilities with high attention to detail


Salary
N150,000 per month.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: cvs@odixcityconsulting.com.ng using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

Procurement Officer Job at Odixcity Consulting




        
    

        
        Odixcity Consulting is an International Hr consulting and procurement firm. With expertise in Recruitment & Selection, Performance management, Training & Development, Compensation and benefits as well as Outsourcing of goods and services for organizations.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Procurement Officer

Job Location: Port Harcourt, Rivers
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

            
    




	We are currently seeking a proactive Procurement Officer to manage end-to-end purchasing activities in our organization. The ideal candidate must have hands-on, proven experience sourcing materials and negotiating vendor contracts within the oil and gas servicing, manufacturing, or marine industries.
	As a Procurement Officer, you will oversee a vital part of our supply chain by evaluating suppliers, negotiating terms, and ensuring the cost-effective procurement of technical equipment, materials, and services. You will play a key role in avoiding operational delays by ensuring that all deliveries meet original specifications and strict industry quality standards.


Job Responsibilities


	Sourcing & Tendering: Prepare proposals, request RFQs (Request for Quotation), and source reliable domestic and foreign vendors.
	Negotiation & Contracting: Negotiate purchase terms, pricing, and delivery schedules to optimize company costs.
	Purchase Orders (POs): Prepare and process POs accurately in accordance with company policies and relevant procurement software.
	Logistics & Expediting: Track placed orders from initial purchase to final delivery in Port Harcourt, ensuring timely arrivals without stockouts.
	Quality Control: Inspect and evaluate the quality of purchased items, resolving any discrepancies or shortcomings with suppliers.
	Record Keeping: Maintain meticulous records of purchasing activities, pricing data, supplier databases, and vendor performance.


Requirements & Skills


	Education: B.Sc / HND in Supply Chain Management, Business Administration, Engineering, or a related discipline.
	Experience: 2–3 years of proven experience in procurement/supply chain within the oil and gas servicing, manufacturing, or marine industry.
	Technical Knowledge: Familiarity with oilfield/marine equipment, materials, and service contracts.
	Regulatory Familiarity: Good understanding of local industry content requirements (e.g., Nigerian Content policies) is a distinct advantage.
	Skills: Exceptional negotiation, analytical, and communication capabilities with high attention to detail


Salary
N150,000 per month.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply
